title: mentha arvensis f. dolichophylla
intro: Mentha arvensis f. dolichophylla, also known as Japanese wild mint or long-leaved wild mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant (Mentha arvensis) that is native to East Asia. This herb is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis f. dolichophylla include its ability to alleviate headaches, colds, and flu, as well as its use as a natural remedy for menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. In herbal preparations, Mentha arvensis f. dolichophylla is often used as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a restful night's sleep.
